# Diff Cover Action

> A GitHub Action for reporting test coverage and code quality only on changed lines in pull requests. Posts PR comments, creates inline annotations, generates badges. Zero vendor lock-in — runs entirely in GitHub Actions.

## What It Does

Diff Cover Action wraps the diff-cover tool to analyze only the lines changed in a pull request, not the entire codebase. It operates in two modes:

- **Coverage mode**: Reports which changed lines lack test coverage. Accepts Cobertura XML, lcov, JaCoCo XML, and Clover XML reports.
- **Quality mode**: Reports lint/style violations only on changed lines. Supports 13+ tools: ruff, flake8, pylint, mypy, eslint, jshint, pycodestyle, pyflakes, checkstyle, cppcheck, shellcheck, pydocstyle, and clang.

## Key Features

- PR comments that update on re-run (idempotent)
- Inline annotations on uncovered or violating lines
- Step summaries in the GitHub Actions UI
- Structured outputs (coverage %, violation count, threshold status) for downstream workflow steps
- Shields.io badge generation
- Automatic shallow clone handling
- Fork PR safe (graceful degradation with read-only tokens)
- Full CLI parity with diff-cover

## Works With Any Language

Any language that produces Cobertura XML, lcov, or JaCoCo coverage reports: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Go, Rust, C#, Ruby, PHP, Kotlin, Swift, and more.

## Usage

```yaml
- uses: Affanmir/diff-cover-action@v1
  with:
    coverage-files: coverage.xml
    fail-under: '80'
```
